jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening sequence crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfectly integrated but routine by this point

jokes.text: Ice cream at church? I'm intrigued, yet suspicious.
jokes.setup: Ice cream being served at church
jokes.punchline: Lisa's analytical response balancing interest and skepticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa characterization - intellectual curiosity balanced with wariness

jokes.text: Blessed Virgin Berry, Command-mint, Bible Gum
jokes.setup: Church ice cream flavors
jokes.punchline: Religious puns on ice cream flavors
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay but predictable religious ice cream puns

jokes.text: Unitarian ice cream - There's nothing here. - Exactly.
jokes.setup: Customer asks about Unitarian ice cream
jokes.punchline: Empty container representing Unitarian theology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant theological satire with perfect economy - the visual gag of emptiness perfectly represents the joke

jokes.text: One Tower of Babel and build it to heaven. To heaven! Oh.
jokes.setup: Bart ordering ice cream in biblical terms
jokes.punchline: Anticlimatic deflation after grandiose biblical reference
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - taking biblical language too far then deflating

jokes.text: I've created the first intra-bovine ice-cream maker. It makes use of all four stomachs.
jokes.setup: Frink introducing his invention
jokes.punchline: Overly scientific description of cow-based ice cream making
jokes.explanation: Classic Frink pseudo-science absurdity with perfect character voice

jokes.text: I'll have the darkety kind.
jokes.setup: Homer ordering ice cream
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up word for chocolate
jokes.explanation: Homer's linguistic invention is endearing if not groundbreaking

jokes.text: [Moos] [Groans] Ivan Reitman!
jokes.setup: Bart getting brain freeze from cow ice cream
jokes.punchline: Bart randomly exclaiming filmmaker's name in pain
jokes.explanation: Completely random pop culture reference in moment of pain - classic Simpsons absurdity

jokes.text: Give me 50 c.c.'s of hot fudge stat!
jokes.setup: Bart's severe brain freeze
jokes.punchline: Medical emergency language applied to ice cream treatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect medical parody with Dr. Hibbert's character - treating brain freeze like surgery

jokes.text: They call her the Christian Madonna... RachelJordan!
jokes.setup: Introduction of Christian entertainer
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic comparison to Madonna in Christian context
jokes.explanation: Good cultural juxtaposition - Madonna being the antithesis of Christian values

jokes.text: [Stammering] I didn't have a crush on her.
jokes.setup: Homer teasing Ned about Rachel
jokes.punchline: Ned's obvious denial and stammering
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy denial - functional but predictable

jokes.text: He can't admit he likes her till he's sure she likes him back. And there's only one way to find out.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining Ned's romantic hesitation
jokes.punchline: Setup for childish solution
jokes.explanation: Sets up the fortune paper gag - good setup work

jokes.text: Does Rachel like Ned? It says I have cooties. Flanders has cooties! Flanders has cooties!
jokes.setup: Homer consulting fortune paper about romance
jokes.punchline: Childish fortune result and crowd reaction
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - adult romantic question answered with playground insult

jokes.text: That was great. I can't believe she found a rhyme for Hezekiah.
jokes.setup: Rachel's Christian performance ending
jokes.punchline: Lisa admiring obscure biblical rhyming challenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - appreciating technical songwriting skill in religious context

jokes.text: We both like plain vanilla with nothin' on it. Actually, all the toppings were gone.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to find romantic connection
jokes.punchline: Deflation of romantic moment with practical reality
jokes.explanation: Good romantic comedy deflation - false connection exposed

jokes.text: Ants are crawling in my mouth, and I don't care.
jokes.setup: Awkward romantic moment continues
jokes.punchline: Ned's oblivious romantic bliss despite discomfort
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd romantic comedy - love conquering literal disgust

jokes.text: All you do is change 'Jesus' to 'baby.'
jokes.setup: Explaining her former band's switch from Christian to pop
jokes.punchline: Revealing simple formula for Christian vs secular music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural observation about the thin line between sacred and secular pop music

jokes.text: Oh, she wants to have coffee sometime! I can hear her, Homer! He can hear you, Rachel!
jokes.setup: Rachel asking Ned for coffee
jokes.punchline: Homer's unnecessary narration and escalating obviousness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer interference - perfect character moment of being helpful in unhelpful way

jokes.text: Why don't you just stay at Ned's place? My place? What would the neighbors think? We're the neighbors, and we don't think.
jokes.setup: Ned worried about neighbors' judgment
jokes.punchline: Homer representing the neighbors and their lack of thought
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with perfect Homer logic - solving Ned's worry by proving neighbors are thoughtless

jokes.text: Now, wait just a Maude-gone minute here!
jokes.setup: Ned getting defensive about moving on from Maude
jokes.punchline: Ned's characteristic word substitution with his dead wife's name
jokes.explanation: Classic Ned speech pattern but with dark undertone of invoking dead wife

jokes.text: Well, you've certainly got a theme going.
jokes.setup: Rachel seeing Ned's house still full of Maude memorabilia
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about obvious shrine-like atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction to obvious visual - understated response to overwhelming shrine

jokes.text: Oh, uh, could you sleep on my side? I'm trying to preserve Maude's indentation.
jokes.setup: Ned offering Rachel his bed
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's preserving dead wife's body impression
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - perfectly captures unhealthy grief in one disturbing detail

jokes.text: How about that? It says 'Maude.' [Sniffs] Mmm, I love chenille. Don't you?
jokes.setup: Ned giving Rachel Maude's blanket
jokes.punchline: Creepy intimate sharing of dead wife's possessions
jokes.explanation: Escalating cringe comedy - Ned's complete obliviousness to how inappropriate this is

jokes.text: Mmm, well, good night, Maude. I mean, Maude. I mean, Rachel! I mean, Maude. Oop!
jokes.setup: Ned saying goodnight to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Multiple slips calling her by dead wife's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating awkwardness - each correction makes it worse

jokes.text: [Gasps, Shrieks] Oh, my God! You're trying to make me look like your dead wife!
jokes.setup: Rachel waking up to see Ned has styled her hair like Maude
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horrified realization of Ned's psychological state
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy payoff - the horrifying realization that crystallizes Ned's problem

jokes.text: No, no! L-l- I know this is, uh, a tad unseemly. But if you just let me even out the back-
jokes.setup: Ned trying to explain his hair styling
jokes.punchline: Ned's complete failure to recognize how disturbing his behavior is
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Ned treating psychotic behavior as minor etiquette issue

jokes.text: We can take whatever we want, right? Don't listen to him. You just have a good time at the eye doctor. Always do!
jokes.setup: Family helping Ned clean out Maude's things
jokes.punchline: Bart's mercenary attitude vs Ned's innocent trust
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Bart's opportunism vs Ned's trusting nature

jokes.text: Yeah, a lifetime of memories. [Grunts] Sure. Of course I do. But life goes on. Ashes to ashes. Turn, turn, turn. Long live rock, and so forth.
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Marge about respect for memories
jokes.punchline: Homer's garbled mix of platitudes and song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer pseudo-wisdom mixing funeral sayings with rock lyrics

jokes.text: Don't throw this away. It's Rod's first tooth. You're right. We could use this for witchcraft.
jokes.setup: Marge sentimentally saving child's tooth
jokes.punchline: Homer's sinister supernatural use for it
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy subversion - innocent keepsake becomes occult ingredient

jokes.text: Wow. Three pairs of shoes. Someone had a fetish.
jokes.setup: Going through Maude's belongings
jokes.punchline: Homer interpreting normal shoe collection as sexual perversion
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment - his crude interpretation of innocent things

jokes.text: I know how to speed things up. [Whining, Grinding] Her pillow's stuck.
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to speed up the sorting process
jokes.punchline: Homer using wood chipper on personal belongings
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy - Homer's inappropriate industrial solution to emotional task

jokes.text: I know how to speed things up. [starts wood chipper]
jokes.setup: Cleaning is taking too long
jokes.punchline: Homer uses a wood chipper to dispose of belongings
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation showing Homer's impatience and disregard for sentimentality

jokes.text: What's that? Some kind of sorting machine? Kinda.
jokes.setup: Ned asks about the wood chipper noise
jokes.punchline: Homer describes the destructive wood chipper as a sorting machine
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual euphemism for destroying Ned's possessions with machinery

jokes.text: Now for the awkward part. We gotta talk about money. You said we were doing this out of friendship. What? That doesn't sound like me.
jokes.setup: Homer bringing up payment for helping
jokes.punchline: Homer denying his own altruistic claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - instantly abandoning altruism for money

jokes.text: Maude designed a Christian amusement park. Oh, it must've been her final dream.
jokes.setup: Discovery of Maude's sketchbook plans
jokes.punchline: Innocent assumption about dead mother's aspirations
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot - children's innocent interpretation drives the story

jokes.text: But to build an amusement park, you need lots of money and manpower and turnstiles. But Mommy wanted it.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining practical obstacles
jokes.punchline: Child's emotional manipulation using dead mother
jokes.explanation: Effective emotional manipulation comedy - child using grief as leverage

jokes.text: They've been out of business ever since that kid got his head cut off. That sounds perfect.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why Storytime Village is available
jokes.punchline: Ned's inappropriate positive response to child death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy juxtaposition - treating child decapitation as real estate opportunity

jokes.text: And what the hell good are you? Beat it, you hippie! But l- You're right. That was harsh.
jokes.setup: Ned admitting he has no money
jokes.punchline: Rich Texan's immediate hostility then false self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Rich Texan's fake self-reflection before continuing meanness

jokes.text: Ah, you are so full of it! God's grace, that is. It's really sickening... there aren't more people like you.
jokes.setup: Rich Texan appearing to insult Ned
jokes.punchline: Multiple misdirections revealing he's actually complimenting
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection comedy - multiple fake-outs before revealing genuine sentiment

jokes.text: Try a little vitamin 'G'! [Gasps] Are you nuts? Relax. It's a controlled burn. Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting gasoline to speed up demolition
jokes.punchline: Homer's overconfidence immediately proven wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overconfidence and immediate failure - 'controlled burn' instantly goes wrong

jokes.text: Darn teenagers with their beer bottles. Uh, yeah. Teenagers. Huh!
jokes.setup: Ned finding beer bottles in construction area
jokes.punchline: Homer's guilty response revealing he's the source
jokes.explanation: Standard comedy of guilt - Homer's transparent deception

jokes.text: But I'm not complaining, 'cause I work for a sweet, sweet boss. You're our foreman, Maude.
jokes.setup: Ned talking to Marge about work
jokes.punchline: Ned addressing dead wife as if she's supervising construction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ned's psychological issues - treating dead wife as construction boss

jokes.text: She's still bossing you around from beyond the grave? Can they do that?
jokes.setup: Homer observing Ned's relationship with dead Maude
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of supernatural management
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - treating psychological issues as actual supernatural powers

jokes.text: Some Chinese people claimed they were celebrating New Year's in February. [Laughs] Oh, yeah. Those guys and their crazy scams.
jokes.setup: Wiggum explaining confiscated fireworks
jokes.punchline: Police treating actual Chinese New Year as suspicious scam
jokes.explanation: Good cultural ignorance comedy - police suspicious of legitimate cultural celebration

jokes.text: Do I detect a hint of cinnamon? Oh, I'll never tell.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about Willie's puke-absorbing sawdust blend
jokes.punchline: Willie treating vomit cleanup recipe as trade secret
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comedy - treating disgusting janitorial mixture as gourmet secret

jokes.text: It is with great pride that I dedicate this new school, sports arena or attraction.
jokes.setup: Mayor Quimby giving dedication speech
jokes.punchline: Generic politician speech admitting ignorance of what he's dedicating
jokes.explanation: Good political satire - politician's generic speech revealing complete disconnect

jokes.text: I hope you enjoy hearing them all. Number one. 'Blessed is the man who does not walk in the council of the wicked.'
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy as King David threatening to recite all 150 psalms
jokes.punchline: Immediate audience exodus at first psalm
jokes.explanation: Great setup/payoff - threatening educational content immediately drives away customers

jokes.text: What do you hit 'em with? There's no mallet. You can stop Satan with your faith.
jokes.setup: Kid at whack-a-mole style Satan game
jokes.punchline: No physical weapon - you use faith, which doesn't work as a game
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of how Christian theme park defeats its own entertainment value through literalism

jokes.text: There's no mallet. You can stop Satan with your faith. My face? You callin' me ugly? No, no, no, no. I think you're beautiful. Oh, that's it!
jokes.setup: Whack-a-Satan game with no mallet
jokes.punchline: Criminal taking religious insult personally and operator's failed diplomacy
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - spiritual warfare game turns into actual violence

jokes.text: Oh, are there two of every flavor? Nope. They're all the same- plain.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about Noah's Ark jellybeans
jokes.punchline: Religious accuracy ruining variety and fun
jokes.explanation: Clever religious accuracy joke - biblical literalism making product worse

jokes.text: [Feminine Voice] I'm Maude. God is super! Can I sit in the car? Let's both sit in the car. Bye-bye.
jokes.setup: Maude masks being sold as merchandise
jokes.punchline: Children's horrified reaction to dead woman masks
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark cringe comedy - commercializing dead wife into creepy merchandise

jokes.text: Can I sit in the car? Let's both sit in the car.
jokes.setup: Bart wants to escape the boring park
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately agrees - they both want out
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective showing how boring the park is - even Lisa wants to leave

jokes.text: Any religion that embraces carob is, uh, not for Carl Carlson.
jokes.setup: Carl complaining about Christian park candy
jokes.punchline: Rejecting religion based on chocolate substitute
jokes.explanation: Great observational comedy about health food in religious context

jokes.text: Oh, Maude, I turned your dream of a Christian amusement park into a bemusement park. It is! It's a bemusement park!
jokes.setup: Ned lamenting his failure to Maude's photo
jokes.punchline: Ned's inadvertent pun about confusing rather than amusing
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay with good character moment of self-awareness

jokes.text: It's a miracle! It's almost like she's alive again.
jokes.setup: Maude statue's face appearing to move
jokes.punchline: Crowd's immediate religious interpretation
jokes.explanation: Standard religious miracle parody - sets up the gas leak revelation

jokes.text: What's that, floating mask? Y-You want me to shoot everyone? Nah, I'm just screwin' with ya. It's a miracle.
jokes.setup: Snake experiencing hallucination
jokes.punchline: Snake joking about mass shooting then going along with miracle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - criminal's violent impulse turned into religious experience

jokes.text: I've never been so close to rubbing my eyes in disbelief. Oh, what the heck! Huh? Huh?
jokes.setup: Moe experiencing miracle
jokes.punchline: Moe's literal interpretation of 'rubbing eyes in disbelief'
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Moe taking idiom literally in moment of wonder

jokes.text: Well, looks like our phenomenon is actually a phenome-not!
jokes.setup: News anchor covering miracle debunking
jokes.punchline: Terrible newscaster pun
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad newscaster pun - comedy comes from the groan factor

jokes.text: It was a golden elementary school with a teacher's lounge that stretched as far as the eye could see. And no one was ever tardy.
jokes.setup: Skinner describing his vision of heaven
jokes.punchline: Principal's heaven is just an idealized school
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific heaven - Skinner's afterlife is perfect bureaucratic control

jokes.text: And no one was ever tardy. Was I there? No! It was heaven- my vision of heaven.
jokes.setup: Skinner's perfect school heaven continues
jokes.punchline: His mother asks if she was there, he says no - because it's HIS heaven
jokes.explanation: Perfect revelation that even in Skinner's ideal afterlife, his mother isn't welcome

jokes.text: Yeah, folks. The power of Christ compels you to give Ned an extra 10 bucks.
jokes.setup: Ned refusing to charge for miracles
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude commercialization of religious experience
jokes.explanation: Good religious commercialization satire with perfect Homer opportunism

jokes.text: [Whimpering] Three is not enough. [Coughing, Gagging]
jokes.setup: Homer mentioning orphanage needing new wall
jokes.punchline: Visual of orphan child suffering from lack of fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark visual gag - literal interpretation of 'needing a wall'

jokes.text: Maude, it's Disco Stu. Lay some heaven on me, foxy dead chick.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu approaching miracle statue
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexualization of dead woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Disco Stu moment - inappropriate behavior with perfect character voice

jokes.text: For me, this is hell. You dig, pally?
jokes.setup: Disco Stu's heaven vision with Frank Sinatra
jokes.punchline: Sinatra's hell is disco music
jokes.explanation: Good musical culture clash - Rat Pack vs disco era conflict

jokes.text: My chair! It's... got a mind of its own! Help me, Mr. Spock! Shazbot! My captain is in peril.
jokes.setup: Professor Frink's wheelchair going crazy from gas
jokes.punchline: He calls for Spock who responds with wrong alien catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi nerd panic with mixed-up alien references

jokes.text: You saved the captain's life. I want to make out with you. And so do Catwoman and Agent 99.
jokes.setup: Professor Frink's Star Trek fantasy heaven
jokes.punchline: Multiple fantasy women wanting to reward him
jokes.explanation: Standard nerd fantasy - predictable but fits Frink's character

jokes.text: That's like explaining how an airplane flies.
jokes.setup: Rod asking why everyone has visions
jokes.punchline: Ned comparing God's will to simple physics
jokes.explanation: Good Ned logic - comparing the incomprehensible to the comprehensible

jokes.text: You better stick your head in and see what's goin' on. Good idea. No, Dad! I think there's a leak in the line!
jokes.setup: Grill won't light despite full gas
jokes.punchline: Homer's dangerous suggestion nearly followed by Ned
jokes.explanation: Standard dangerous Homer advice - Lisa saves the day with logic

jokes.text: Oh, no! This isn't a divine miracle. Everyone's just gettin' goofy from the gas.
jokes.setup: Ned discovering the gas leak
jokes.punchline: Deflating religious experience with scientific explanation
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment - religious vs scientific explanation conflict

jokes.text: That- How could that be worse? Okay, permanent brain damage or just temporary?
jokes.setup: Ned calling gas company about toxicity
jokes.punchline: Each answer making situation worse
jokes.explanation: Good escalating phone call - each clarification worse than the last

jokes.text: 'Could' get hurt. 'Could.' There's a chance they won't.
jokes.setup: Ned wanting to close the park for safety
jokes.punchline: Homer's optimistic interpretation of danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - finding optimism in statistical probability of harm

jokes.text: Orphans lighting candles over a leaking gas line. Lighting candles?
jokes.setup: Homer listing positive effects of the park
jokes.punchline: Realization that candles plus gas leak equals explosion
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy buildup - beautiful moment turns horrifying

jokes.text: Adults attacking orphans? I don't think Maude Flanders would approve of that. Absolutely not!
jokes.setup: Gas-crazed adults attacking orphans
jokes.punchline: Crowd's moral judgment while participating in violence
jokes.explanation: Good dark irony - mob suddenly developing moral standards mid-violence

jokes.text: Adults attacking orphans? I don't think Maude Flanders would approve of that.
jokes.setup: Gas-crazed adults fighting injured orphans
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about Maude's likely opinion
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan journalism about obviously inappropriate violence

jokes.text: Yeah. This place is more like 'Crazyland.' Instead of 'Praiseland.' I see. It's a play on words.
jokes.setup: Homer makes pun about the failed park
jokes.punchline: Crowd needs the wordplay explained
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that the show acknowledges is weak through crowd's confusion

jokes.text: Yeah. This place is more like 'Crazyland.' Instead of 'Praiseland.' I see. It's a play on words.
jokes.setup: Moe making pun about the park's name
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy explaining the obvious pun
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about explaining obvious wordplay - Comic Book Guy's pedantry

jokes.text: Not! [Laughing] Hey, your hair's grown back real nice. It's a wig. And let's never speak of it again.
jokes.setup: Homer joking about Ned not liking Rachel
jokes.punchline: Awkward reference to her hair being styled like Maude's
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier trauma with appropriately awkward resolution

jokes.text: Hey, your hair's grown back real nice. It's a wig. And let's never speak of it again.
jokes.setup: Ned comments on Rachel's hair
jokes.punchline: She reveals it's a wig from when he cut it off
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the hair-cutting incident with appropriate awkwardness
